texto: If we regularly ponder the depth of our own sinfulness, like the woman who washed Jesus' feet with her tears, and consider the greater depth of God's forgiveness, we will grow in our love for him. It is when we forget God's benefits that our hearts become proud. If we are not careful, we who have been forgiven much can act like those who think they have been forgiven little and, consequently, become slow to forgive others who sin against us.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Paul Tautges: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “If we regularly ponder the depth of our own sinfulness, like the woman who washed Jesus' feet wit…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, forgiveness, sin pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de sin.

texto: As long as the eyes of our faith are fixed upon our troubles we will not find joy. Until our minds consciously move the Lord from our peripheral vision into the narrow corridor of our focus, joy will elude us.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Tautges sobre temas ligados a fe. “As long as the eyes of our faith are fixed upon our troubles we will not find joy. Until our mind…” articula fe, faith-in-god, joy em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como joy.

texto: Just as the expansion and contraction of our lungs in necessary for the continuance of our physical life, so regular fellowship with God in prayer is essential to our spiritual well-being. Without prayer our spiritual lives will shrivel up and return to an infantile state.
explicacao: Citação de Paul Tautges sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. “Just as the expansion and contraction of our lungs in necessary for the continuance of our physic…” articula fe, essentials, prayer em registro de fe, com relevância para contextos como prayer e spiritual.

texto: When we find ourselves praying for something that we want, perhaps more than anything we've ever wanted before, can we honestly pray, 'Lord, do what will bring you the most glory"?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Paul Tautges que provoca temas ligados a reflexao. Ao questionar “When we find ourselves praying for something that we want, perhaps more than anything we've ever …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, prayer, ourselves, com foco em reflexao.
